content: Open messages with a single click in the message pane instead of using the double-click to open it in a new tab. If you prefer to read messages in tabs, close all tabs before you quit Thunderbird: R-click on Mail tab > Close other tabs
creator: Mapenzi

content: When we start the application, all messages are open as tabs. Closing the tabs does not prevent them being open next time we start Thunderbird.
creator: rob.faivre

content: Thunderbird on startup automatically displays all tabs which had been left open on closing the previous session. Go to menu Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Open Folder, then quit Thunderbird. In the profile folder search for the session.json file and rename it in session-old.json When you restart Thunderbird a new session.json file will be created and your open tabs from the previous session should have disappeared.
creator: Mapenzi

I would have thought the text was clear, so I am guessing it is the background you don't have.

Encrypted connections (SSL/TLS mostly) use certificates issued in a chain of trust to encrypt the communications betwen ou and them. Your providers certificate has expired so Thunderbird is not playing. Ring them and advise them to get with it. They have a problem.
